evals.prompt: Create a rackula layout file for my homelab with a 42U 4-post-cabinet rack that has a Dell R650 1U server at U42, a Ubiquiti 48-port PoE switch at U40, and two 4U storage NAS devices at U30-33 and U26-29. Also add a 2U APC UPS at the bottom (U1-2) with va_rating of 3000 and a 24-port patch panel at U3. Include proper device_types with manufacturer, model, part_number, interfaces, and power_ports for the UPS.
evals.expected_output: Valid .rackula.yaml file with all device types properly defined including component arrays
expectations: Uses modern top-level racks array, not legacy rack object
Places requested devices using modern internal 1/6U positions
Full-depth server, storage, and UPS placements use face: both so they appear in rear view
Defines device types for Dell server, Ubiquiti switch, NAS, APC UPS, and patch panel
UPS device type includes va_rating 3000 and at least one power port
Device type slugs are unique and valid kebab-case

evals.prompt: I need a rack layout file for a 2-post 20U wall-mount rack with 5 generic 1U servers stacked starting at U1, a 24-port patch panel at U6, and a 1U switch at U7 with front-to-rear airflow. Make the switch half-depth (is_full_depth: false). Add a blank 2U panel at U8-9 to fill space.
evals.expected_output: Valid .rackula.yaml file with wall-mount form_factor and half-depth device
expectations: Uses modern top-level racks array with a 20U rack
Uses wall-mount form_factor for the requested wall-mount rack
Places servers U1-U5, patch panel U6, switch U7, and blank U8 using internal positions
Server placements use face: both when rear view should show installed equipment
Switch device type is half-depth and front-to-rear airflow
Blank panel is 2U, category blank, and gray #44475A

evals.prompt: Add a new blade chassis to my-layout.rackula.yaml - put a 4U blade chassis at position 120 with 4 device bays. Then add 2 blade servers inside it at Bay 1 and Bay 2 with names 'Production Blade 1' and 'Production Blade 2'. The chassis should have colour #8BE9FD and category chassis, the blades should have colour #BD93F9.
evals.expected_output: Modified YAML with parent/child device relationship properly configured using container_id/slot_id model
expectations: Uses modern top-level racks array
Adds a 4U blade chassis device type with slots array and category chassis
Adds blade server device type with requested colour
Adds two named blade placements using container_id and slot_id
Container children use position 0 (relative to container) not rack-level positions

evals.prompt: Create a rackula file for a small office server room with a 42U open-frame rack containing: a 3U UPS at the bottom with va_rating 5000, nema-l5-30p input type and 4500W maximum_draw; two 2U servers at U4-5 and U6-7 (front-to-rear airflow, 750W power ports); a 1U switch at U8 with side-to-rear airflow and 48 gigabit interfaces plus one 10G uplink interface. Include serial numbers, asset tags, and notes for each device.
evals.expected_output: Complete rackula file with power calculations, detailed interfaces, and extension fields
expectations: Uses modern top-level racks array with 42U open-frame rack
Places UPS, servers, and switch at requested internal positions
UPS has va_rating 5000 and nema-l5-30p input with 4500 maximum_draw
Switch defines 48 gigabit interfaces plus one 10G uplink interface
Device types include serial numbers, asset tags, and notes

evals.prompt: I have a rackula file called my-layout.rackula.yaml. Change the display_mode to 'image-label' and set show_labels_on_images to true. Also add a custom_field called 'department' with value 'infrastructure' to the rack's notes or metadata.
evals.expected_output: Modified settings section with display_mode change and custom_fields added
expectations: Sets settings.display_mode to image-label
Sets settings.show_labels_on_images to true
Adds department custom field with value infrastructure to metadata or a rack
Preserves racks and device_types from the original layout

evals.prompt: Create a rackula layout with two 42U racks in a row. Rack 1 has a 4U server at U20 and a 1U switch at U18. Rack 2 has two 2U servers at U10-11 and U12-13, plus a 1U patch panel at U14. Add a rack_group called 'Main Row' that groups both racks. Use connections to link the switch in Rack 1 to the patch panel in Rack 2 via port IDs.
evals.expected_output: Valid rackula YAML with rack groups, multi-rack layout, and connections
expectations: Uses modern racks array with two rack objects
Places devices at correct internal positions for each rack
rack_groups section defines 'Main Row' with both rack IDs
connections array links switch ports to patch panel ports using a_port_id and b_port_id
Does not use connections[].from or connections[].to
Does not use nested device_id/port_id endpoint objects inside connections
Validates that every a_port_id and b_port_id exists in racks[].devices[].ports[].id
device_types defined for all placed device types

evals.prompt: Create a rackula file for a 10U half-width open-frame rack with 3 half-width mini PCs (slot_width: 1) at U1, U3, and U5 - all using left slot_position. Add a 1U full-width patch panel at U7 on the right side (slot_position: right) to fill the remaining space. Use colour #50FA7B for the mini PCs.
evals.expected_output: Valid rackula YAML with half-width devices properly positioned
expectations: Uses 10U rack with open-frame form_factor
Mini PC device types have slot_width: 1 and category server
Mini PC placements use slot_position: left
Patch panel uses slot_position: right and is full-width
Devices placed at correct internal positions for 10U rack

evals.prompt: Package the rackula YAML files in ./input into importable Rackula ZIP files in ./output. Assume the rackula skill is installed but the original development repo is not cloned, so package.json scripts may not exist.
evals.expected_output: Uses the bundled skill script directly with Bun first and Node as fallback
expectations: Uses .agents/skills/rackula/scripts/zip-yaml.js directly
Uses .agents/skills/rackula/scripts/validate-rackula.js for structural validation
Can run the bundled validator with node because its YAML parser is included in the generated script
Does not use ad hoc Node snippets requiring the yaml package in the user workspace
Tries bun run before node when Bun is available
Falls back to node if bun is unavailable
Uses --file instead of --input when packaging one named YAML file
Uses --input only when packaging all YAML files in a directory
Recognizes --output, --name, --text, --stdout, and --help options
Does not run pnpm run build or pnpm run zip-yaml
Does not require package.json or a cloned development workspace
Uses explicit --input and --output flags
